---
title: "Lauren and Jillian EDA"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

# GIT COMMANDS
# git add _____/
# git commit -m "Message"
# git push origin main

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(leaps)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmnet)
library(ISLR)
library(dials)
library(tidymodels)
```

```{r}
# Load data
final_train_lags <- read_csv("/data/accounts/fa24/garretthayden/DSCI445/Group3FinalProject/Data//final_train_lags.csv")
```


```{r}
# Step 3): Perform Lasso to choose lags

# Actually may not need, since we are already performing "manual feature selection"

final_train_lagged_sales <- final_train_lagged_sales %>%
  drop_na(sales_lag_1, sales_lag_7, sales_lag_30, sales_lag_60, sales_lag_90, sales_lag_180, sales_lag_365)

lag_recipe <- recipe(sales ~ sales_lag_1 + sales_lag_7 + sales_lag_30 + sales_lag_60 + sales_lag_90 + sales_lag_180 + sales_lag_365, data = final_train_lagged_sales) %>%
  step_normalize(all_predictors())

cv <- vfold_cv(final_train_lagged_sales, v = 10)
lambda_grid <- grid_regular(penalty(), levels = 50)

lasso_spec <- linear_reg(mixture = 1, penalty = tune()) %>%
  set_mode("regression") %>%
  set_engine("glmnet")

lasso_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(lasso_spec) %>%
  add_recipe(lag_recipe)

lasso_tune <- lasso_workflow %>%
  tune_grid(
    resamples = cv,
    grid = lambda_grid,
    metrics = metric_set(rmse)
  )

lasso_final <- finalize_workflow(lasso_workflow, select_best(lasso_tune, metric = "rmse"))
lasso_fin_fit <- fit(lasso_final, data = final_train_lagged_sales)

results <- collect_metrics(lasso_tune)
results_rmse <- results %>% filter(.metric == "rmse")

ggplot(results_rmse, aes(x = penalty, y = mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = expression(lambda), y = "CV Error (Mean RMSE)", title = "CV Error vs. Lambda")

best_lambda <- show_best(lasso_tune, metric = "rmse", n = 1)
lasso_coef <- tidy(lasso_fin_fit)
cor(final_train_lagged_sales %>% select(sales, starts_with("sales_lag_")))

lasso_coef
```
